name: Malik ibn Nusayr
title: Looter of the Cap
shortDescription: The one who struck Imam Hussain's (AS) head with a sword on Ashura and looted his cap (Burnus).
fullStory:
Malik ibn Nusayr al-Kindi was one of the ruthless fighters in the Kufan army. On Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) was exhausted from fighting and was wearing a cap (Burnus), Malik ibn Nusayr charged forward.

He struck a heavy blow with his sword on Imam Hussain's (AS) head. The blow split the Imam's cap, the sword reached his holy head, and blood flowed. The Imam cursed him, saying: 'May God deprive you of eating and drinking, and gather you with the oppressors.'

Malik ibn Nusayr looted the Imam's blood-stained cap and took it to his wife to wash, but his wife, who was a believing woman, yelled at him: 'Have you brought the Prophet's grandson's cap to my house? Take it out!'

The Imam's curse upon him was answered. He lived in poverty and misery until the end of his life, and both his hands became paralyzed, until he was eventually captured and punished by Mukhtar.
quoteArabic: لا أَكَلْتَ بِها وَلا شَرِبْتَ، وَحَشَرَكَ اللَّهُ مَعَ الظّالِمِينَ...
quote: (Imam Hussain's curse upon him:) May you never eat or drink with it, and may God gather you with the oppressors.
